q: A compound microscope uses lenses to make ___ images of objects that are too small for the unaided eye to see.
A common type of microscope is a ___ microscope.
The lens nearer the object is called the ___.
The lens through which the final image is viewed is called the ___.
The objective produces an image which is real, ___ and larger than the size of the object.
The final image formed is virtual and on the same ___ as the object.
The final image can be seen clearly when the eye is very close to the ___.
The separation of the lenses in a microscope is $L = v_o + ___$.
The magnifying power ($MP$) of a compound microscope is the product of the magnification of the objective and the magnifying power of the ___.
The separation of the lenses in a compound microscope is $L = v_o + ___$.
When the final image is formed at infinity, the condition is $u_e = ___$.
For a large magnifying power of a compound microscope both the objective and the eyepiece should have ___ focal lengths.
Electron microscopes deliver a more detailed image compared to ___ microscopes.
Optical microscopes use ___ or light energy.
Electron microscopes use electrons, which have shorter wavelengths that allows greater ___.
There are two main types of electron microscope: the Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM) and the Transmission Electron Microscope (___).
The image of the specimen in an electron microscope may be viewed by projecting the magnified electron image onto a fluorescent viewing ___.
Electron microscopes are used to investigate the ultrastructure of a wide range of biological and inorganic specimens, metals, and ___.
